AP Calculus

1.12 Confirming Continuity over an Interval

Opening

AP CALCULUS AB · UNIT 1

1.12 Confirming Continuity over an Interval

Use domains, endpoint conditions, and continuity theorems to justify an entire interval—not merely one point.

acb
continuous on each connected piece

Learning Outcomes

Learning outcomes

2

A formula may be continuous everywhere it is defined, but the interval notation must show every restriction and endpoint correctly.

1Distinguish continuity on open, closed, and half-open intervals.

2Use one-sided continuity correctly at endpoints.

3Find maximal continuity intervals from domains and restrictions.

4Justify continuity of operations and compositions on stated intervals.

Vocabulary

Four keywords

3
Maximal intervalA largest connected interval on which the function is continuous.
Endpoint continuityRight-continuity at a left endpoint or left-continuity at a right endpoint.
Domain restrictionAn input excluded by a denominator, logarithm, radical, or trigonometric condition.
CompositionA function built by feeding the output of one continuous function into another.

Warm-Up

Warm-up · Where can a journey continue?

4

Imagine tracing the graph without lifting your finger. On which input intervals can you travel without crossing a break?

  • Name the connected pieces using interval notation.
  • Decide whether the break point belongs to either piece.
  • Explain what evidence would be needed at an endpoint.

Warm-Up

Warm-up debrief · Connected pieces first

5

A continuity interval must be connected and must contain only inputs where the function satisfies the appropriate continuity condition.

A missing point splits one interval into two, even when the graph is otherwise smooth.

Activity · Interior points versus endpoints

6
1

Inside the interval

At every , require .
2

At the left endpoint

At the right endpoint

State the interval

Use brackets only when the required endpoint value exists and matches the one-sided limit.

Progress 1

Definition · Continuity on an interval

7

means two-sided continuity at every interior point, right-continuity at the left endpoint, and left-continuity at the right endpoint.

  • : ordinary two-sided continuity.
  • : only inputs from the right are available.
  • : only inputs from the left are available.

Progress 1

Solved activity · Endpoint ownership

8

Example A

Square-root endpoint

1

Domain

includes the left endpoint.
2

Interior

The square-root family is continuous throughout its domain.
3

Endpoint

.
Conclusion: continuous on .

Example B

Reciprocal endpoint

1

Domain

throughout the interval.
2

Interior

A rational function is continuous wherever its denominator is nonzero.
3

Endpoints

The appropriate one-sided limits equal the endpoint values.
Conclusion: continuous on .

Progress 1

Try it · Audit a closed interval

9

Is the function continuous on the entire stated interval? If not, replace it with the maximal continuity intervals inside the domain.

  • Check the radical endpoint.
  • Locate every denominator zero.
  • Use bracket or parenthesis notation deliberately.

Progress 2

Activity · Domain detective

11

requires .

requires .

requires .

requires .

For each restriction, say whether the boundary is included, excluded, or must be split into repeated intervals.

Progress 2

Explanation · Standard families are continuous on their domains

12

Polynomials, rational functions, radicals, logarithms, exponentials, and trigonometric functions inherit continuity wherever their defining operations are valid.

The hard work is often finding the domain correctly.

Progress 2

Solved activity · Intersect the restrictions

13
1

Logarithm

gives .
2

Denominator radical

gives ; equality is excluded because the root is in the denominator.
3

Intersection

.
4

Continuity theorem

Each component is continuous on its domain, so the quotient is continuous on the intersection.
Maximal interval: .

Choose a family, then scan its restrictions.
Maximal intervals will appear here.

Progress 3

Activity · A piecewise graph can be continuous

16

Read the left-hand limit, right-hand limit, and point value at the join. Does the graph pass all three continuity conditions?

Progress 3

Explanation · Piecewise boundaries are the only new checkpoints

17

Each formula may already be continuous on its own region. To justify continuity over a larger interval, inspect every boundary where the formula changes.

Progress 3

Solved activity · Verify two boundaries

18
1

At zero

Left limit ; right limit and value . They differ, so continuity fails.
2

At two

Left limit ; right limit and value . Continuity holds.
3

Intervals

Split at every discontinuity and preserve endpoints where one-sided continuity holds.
Maximal continuity intervals: and . The join at is continuous, so the two right-hand pieces combine into one maximal interval.

Try it · From formula to interval notation

19

Determine every maximal interval on which g is continuous. Include a one-sided check at x=−1 and a join check at x=3.

Progress 4

Activity · Build continuity from operations

21
1

Start with known continuous functions

Identify the domain on which each component is continuous.
2

Apply an operation

Sums and products preserve continuity; quotients also require a nonzero denominator.
3

Check an outer function

For , verify that the range of stays inside the continuity domain of .
4

State the exact interval

Intersect all conditions and split at excluded inputs.

Progress 5

Activity · Full interval audit

26

Create a restriction ledger before writing intervals.

  • Radical condition
  • Logarithm condition
  • Denominator zeros
  • Periodic cosine zeros
  • Connected components

Progress 5

Explanation · Maximal means no legal extension

27

An interval is maximal when it cannot be enlarged in either direction without including an input where the function is undefined or discontinuous.

Progress 5

Solved activity · Rational, radical, and periodic restrictions

28
1

Radical

.
2

Linear denominator

Exclude .
3

Cosine denominator

Exclude .
4

Assemble

Order every excluded input above −5 and write the connected pieces, including −5 as a valid left endpoint.
Key idea: a periodic restriction produces infinitely many maximal intervals.

Progress 5

Try it · Claim audit

29

A student writes “continuous on its domain” but lists only one interval for tan x. What is the strongest correction?

The domain has infinitely many connected components; report continuity on each component.

Challenge · Dense branches and isolated repairs

31

Let

Both rational and irrational inputs occur arbitrarily close to every target.

Determine every point at which f is continuous. Your argument must compare both dense branches.

Challenge

Challenge · Construct the continuity set

33

Construct a function continuous exactly on (−∞,−1) and [2,∞), but discontinuous at every input in [−1,2). State a clear formula and justify the two continuity pieces.

20questions

Beyond AP

Beyond AP · Local continuity controls nearby behavior

35

If a continuous function is positive at one input, it remains positive throughout some sufficiently small neighborhood.

This is a local consequence of continuity, not a global monotonicity claim.

Beyond AP

Beyond AP · Continuous functions under max and min

36

Use the identity to explain why the maximum of two continuous functions is continuous. Then write the analogous formula for the minimum.

Exit Ticket

Exit ticket · State, restrict, justify

39

1State the endpoint continuity condition at x=−2.

2Find the maximal interval of continuity of √(x+2)/(x−1).

3Explain why the interval must split at x=1.

4Name one theorem used in your justification.
